flashcardDecks.name: Math Formulas
flashcardDecks.description: Essential mathematics formulas for Grade 12 NSC including algebra, trigonometry, calculus, and geometry

flashcardDecks.name: Physics Definitions
flashcardDecks.description: Key physics concepts and formulas for Grade 12 physical sciences including mechanics, waves, and electricity

flashcardDecks.name: Chemistry Reactions
flashcardDecks.description: Important chemical reactions, periodic trends, and organic chemistry for NSC Grade 12

flashcardDecks.name: Life Sciences
flashcardDecks.description: Cell biology, genetics, evolution, and human physiology for Grade 12 life sciences

flashcardDecks.name: English Literature
flashcardDecks.description: Literary devices, essay writing terms, and comprehension strategies for English home language

flashcardDecks.name: History Key Events
flashcardDecks.description: Important historical events, figures, and movements for South African and world history

flashcards.back: d/dx(x^n) = nx^(n-1)
a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C
c² = a² + b² - 2ab cos C
Area = ½ ab sin C
sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
sin 2θ = 2 sin θ cos θ
cos 2θ = cos²θ - sin²θ = 2cos²θ - 1 = 1 - 2sin²θ
S_n = a(r^n - 1)/(r - 1) for r ≠ 1
A = P(1 + i)^n
(x - a)² + (y - b)² = r²
y = mx + c where m is gradient and c is y-intercept
d/dx(uv) = u'v + uv'
d/dx(u/v) = (u'v - uv')/v²
log_a(x) = log(x)/log(a) = ln(x)/ln(a)
d/dx(e^x) = e^x
d/dx(ln x) = 1/x
∫_a^b f(x)dx = F(b) - F(a)
cos²θ = (1 + cos 2θ)/2
sin²θ = (1 - cos 2θ)/2
2 sin A cos B = sin(A + B) + sin(A - B)
2 cos A cos B = cos(A + B) + cos(A - B)
x² + bx + c = (x + b/2)² - (b/2)² + c
An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force
F = ma (Force equals mass times acceleration)
For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction
v = u + at (final velocity = initial velocity + acceleration × time)
s = ut + ½at² (displacement = initial velocity × time + ½ × acceleration × time²)
v² = u² + 2as (final velocity squared = initial velocity squared + 2 × acceleration × displacement)
KE = ½mv² (Kinetic energy = ½ × mass × velocity squared)
GPE = mgh (mass × gravitational acceleration × height)
W = Fd cos θ (Work = force × displacement × cos of angle between force and displacement)
P = W/t = Fv (Power = work done / time = force × velocity)
v = fλ (velocity = frequency × wavelength)
T = 1/f (Period = 1 / frequency)
V = IR (Voltage = current × resistance)
P = IV = I²R = V²/R (Power = current × voltage)
p = mv (momentum = mass × velocity)
m₁u₁ + m₂u₂ = m₁v₁ + m₂v₂ (total momentum before = total momentum after)
J = FΔt = Δp (Impulse = force × time = change in momentum)
F = Gm₁m₂/r² (Force = gravitational constant × mass1 × mass2 / distance²)
E = F/q = kQ/r² (Electric field = force / charge)
F = -kx (Force = -spring constant × extension)
P = F/A (Pressure = force / area)
f' = f(v ± vo)/(v ∓ vs) (observed frequency changes with relative motion)
1/f = 1/do + 1/di (1/focal length = 1/object distance + 1/image distance)
n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂ (refraction angle depends on refractive indices)
Q = mcΔT (heat = mass × specific heat capacity × temperature change)
Efficiency = (useful output / input) × 100%
EPE = ½kx² (elastic potential energy = ½ × spring constant × extension²)
Law of Conservation of Mass: atoms cannot be created or destroyed
M = n/V (Molarity = moles of solute / volume of solution in dm³)
6.022 × 10²³ particles per mole
Increases from left to right across a period, decreases down a group
Transfer of electrons from metal to non-metal, electrostatic attraction
Sharing of electron pairs between non-metals
Sea of delocalized electrons surrounded by positive metal ions
Free elements = 0, O = -2, H = +1, metals = positive, sum = 0
pH = -log[H⁺] (measure of hydrogen ion concentration)
Temperature, concentration, surface area, catalyst, pressure (for gases)
System at equilibrium will shift to counteract any change applied
CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ (saturated hydrocarbons with single bonds)
CₙH₂ₙ (unsaturated hydrocarbons with double bond)
Unsaturated compounds add atoms across double/triple bonds
One atom/group replaces another in a molecule
Fuel + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O + energy (complete combustion produces CO₂ and H₂O)
Breaking down of electrolyte using electrical energy
Simultaneous oxidation (electron loss) and reduction (electron gain)
Molar mass = sum of atomic masses in the formula
PV = nRT (Pressure × Volume = moles × gas constant × Temperature)
n = m/M (moles = mass / molar mass)
% element = (atomic mass × number of atoms / molar mass) × 100
Simplest whole number ratio of atoms in a compound
Actual number of atoms in a molecule (multiple of empirical formula)
ΔH = H_products - H_reactants (heat absorbed/released in reaction)
Minimum energy required for a reaction to occur
Substance that lowers activation energy without being consumed
Site of cellular respiration, produces ATP (the powerhouse of the cell)
Contains DNA, controls cell activities and reproduction
Site of protein synthesis (translation)
Phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins (fluid mosaic model)
Chromatin condenses into chromosomes, nuclear envelope breaks down
Chromosomes align at the cell equator, spindle fibers attach
Sister chromatids separate and move to opposite poles
Nuclear envelopes reform, chromosomes uncoil, cytoplasm divides
Double helix, antiparallel strands, complementary base pairing (A-T, G-C)
mRNA (messenger), tRNA (transfer), rRNA (ribosomal)
Semi-conservative, DNA polymerase adds nucleotides 5' to 3'
DNA sequence copied to mRNA in the nucleus
mRNA code read at ribosome to build protein (amino acid sequence)
Allele pairs separate during gamete formation
Genes for different traits assort independently during gamete formation
Allele that masks the recessive allele, expressed in heterozygotes
Allele masked by dominant allele, only expressed in homozygotes
Both alleles expressed equally in heterozygote (e.g., AB blood type)
Heterozygote shows intermediate phenotype (e.g., pink snapdragons)
Organisms with favorable traits survive and reproduce more successfully
Change in inherited characteristics of a population over time
Formation of new species through evolution
6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂
C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ATP
Site of photosynthesis, contains chlorophyll
Direct comparison without using 'like' or 'as' (e.g., 'All the world's a stage')
Comparison using 'like' or 'as' (e.g., 'brave as a lion')
Giving human traits to non-human things
Repetition of consonant sounds at the start of words (e.g., 'Sally sells seashells')
Exaggeration for emphasis or effect (e.g., 'I'm so hungry I could eat a horse')
Words that imitate sounds (e.g., 'buzz', 'hiss', 'crash')
Contrast between expectation and reality (verbal, dramatic, or situational)
Central idea or message of a literary work
Recurring element that contributes to the theme
Person telling the story (first person, third person limited, omniscient)
Character who contrasts with main character to highlight traits
Main character in a story
Character opposing the protagonist
Main argument of an essay, usually in the introduction
Point, Evidence, Explanation - organize paragraph arguments
Using objects to represent ideas or qualities
Story with hidden meaning, often political or moral
Hints about future events in the story
Scene from the past interrupting present action
Drawing conclusions based on evidence and reasoning
Four British colonies unified, laying foundation for apartheid
System of racial segregation implemented by National Party
Police fired on protesters, 69 killed, led to ANC armed struggle
Student protest against Afrikaans medium education, Hector Petersen killed
Anti-apartheid leader, imprisoned 27 years, first democratically elected President
Black Consciousness Movement founder, died in police custody 1977
Multi-party negotiation forum to end apartheid
First democratic election in South Africa, Mandela elected President
Global conflict triggered by assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
Global conflict involving Axis and Allied powers
Ideological conflict between USA (capitalism) and USSR (communism)
Overthrow of Tsarist regime, establishment of Soviet state
End of British rule, partition into India and Pakistan
Overthrow of monarchy, rise of radical politics in France
Movement advocating solidarity among African peoples
Founded 1912, led anti-apartheid struggle
1995-1996, chaired by Desmond Tutu, addressed apartheid human rights violations
Indian independence leader, pioneered nonviolent resistance
1959 revolution led by Fidel Castro, established communist state
Wave of African nations gaining independence in 1960s
Universal rights inherent to all humans regardless of nationality or status
Patriotic feeling or loyalty to one's nation
Policy of extending a nation's power through colonization or force
Thousands of students marched against forced Afrikaans education, police opened fire
ANC leaders including Mandela sentenced to life imprisonment at Liliesleaf Farm
Movement founded by Steve Biko to restore black South Africans' dignity
